data.title: IPO Roundup: Anbio Biotechnology, Wellgistics Health and more
data.description:
The U.S. IPO activity looks sluggish amid the holiday-shortened week. Nevertheless, the week prior saw Texas-based cybersecurity firm SailPoint price its upsized IPO of 60M shares at a public offering price of $23.00 per share.

Additionally, shares of defense contractor Karman Holdings (KRMN) saw their shares rise by nearly 50% after its public offering, as investors liked the positioning of this missile, defense & space business.

This week, Germany-based Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is expected to raise capital during its IPO.

"NNNN is seeking US public capital market investment as it aims to commercialize its in vitro diagnostics products," said SA Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones in a recent article on Anbio Biotechnology.

"The market opportunity for providing IVD products worldwide is large and is expected to grow at a moderate rate of growth in the coming years."

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is raising $8.8M by offering 1.6M shares at a price range of about $5.00 and $6.00. Description: Medical technology company.
  • Wellgistics Health (WGRX) is raising $4.5M by offering 0.9M shares at a price range of about $4.50 and $5.50. Description: Holding company.
  • Apimeds Pharmaceuticals (APUS) is raising $11.7M by offering 3.3M shares at a price range of about $3.00 and $4.00. Description: Clinical biopharmaceutical company.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • Crane Harbor Acquisition (CHACU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $175M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • Envoy Technologies (EVOY) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $12M in an initial public offering. Description: Electric car-sharing firm.
  • Harvard Ave Acquisition (HAVAU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $250M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • UY Scuti Acquisition (UYSCU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $50M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • Kestra Medical Technologies (KMTS) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $100M in an initial public offering. Description: Wearable cardiac device maker.
  • Robot Consulting (LAWR) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $13M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 2.5M ADSs priced between $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Japanese HR platform provider.
  • Qinhui Technology International (QHT) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $7M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 1.3M shares priced between $5.00 and $6.00. Description: Chinese auto parts maker.

Withdrawals

  • Turo has withdrawn its plans for an initial public offering. Previously, the company had filed in 2022 with the SEC to raise about $300M.

Lock-up period expirations

  • Reitar Logtech (RITR) on 2/19/2025. Price movement over the past six months: XX%
  • WORK Medical Technology Group (WOK) on 2/19/2025. Price movement over the past six months: XX%

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has risen about 7.1%, outperforming the S&P 500 index, which has gained about 4.0%.

data.title: Cargojet reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cargojet press release (OTCPK:CGJTF): Q4 Total revenue was C$293.2M compared to fourth quarter 2023 revenue of C$221.9M.
  • Q4 Adjusted EPS C$1.71

data.title: Waste Energy secures up to $0.75M non-dilutive financing commitment
data.description:
• Waste Energy (OTCQB:WAST) has successfully closed the first tranche of $175K of a non-dilutive issuance of non-convertable preferred shares financing of up to $750K to complete its first waste-to-energy conversion facility in Fayetteville, North Carolina.
  • The capital will be used to finalize the build-out of the site, which is expected to be fully operational by June 1, 2025.
  • By developing this first facility, the company is demonstrating the commercial viability of its technology and paving the way for future expansion.
  • "The Fayetteville facility is expected to generate between $1.5-$5M in revenue in its first full year of operation, this facility positions the company for strong financial growth while advancing its sustainable energy solutions." said Waste Energy Corp CEO, Scott Gallagher.

data.title: AppTech Payments announces extension for continued Nasdaq listing
data.description:
• AppTech Payments (NASDAQ:APCX) received notice from Nasdaq that its listing will continue, provided it meets certain conditions.
  • These include filing a public disclosure by March 31, 2025, confirming the shareholders' equity deficiency is cured and detailing this in its 2023 10-K, expected by March 15, 2025. 
  • The company was also given until May 5, 2025, to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid price per share requirement.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Jushi Holdings receives $5.1 million of factored ERC refund claim proceeds
data.description:
• Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF) on Tuesday said it has received $5.1 million of proceeds in connection with the factoring of certain employee retention credits (ERC) refund claims and separately received binding subscriptions to sell additional 12% second lien notes due 2026 under its existing indenture for aggregate net proceeds of $4.6 million.
  • The company said its certain affiliates sold approximately $6 million of United States ERC refund claims to a third party for approximately $5.1 million of net cash proceeds. Certain affiliates of the company have approximately $3 million of additional United States ERC refund claims remaining with the United States Internal Revenue Service, which were not sold as part of this transaction.
  • The company also received binding subscriptions to sell about $5.1 million aggregate principal amount of second lien notes, from which the company expects to receive net cash proceeds of around $4.6 million.
  • The second Lien Notes will be issued at a 10.00% original issuance discount. The closing will occur at least five business days following the date of this press release.
  • The company said an entity affiliated with James Cacioppo, the company’s chief executive officer, chairman and founder, has subscribed for approximately $3.7 million principal amount of the United States dollar denominated Second Lien Notes, for a purchase price of approximately $3.35 million and will receive up to approximately 6.2 million Warrants.
  • Denis Arsenault, a Founder and significant equity holder of the company, has subscribed for C$2 million principal amounts of Canadian dollar denominated Second Lien Notes, for a purchase price of C$1.8 million and will receive up to approximately 2.4 million warrants.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: W.P. Carey upgraded at BMO Capital on growth, incremental cost of capital
data.description:
Net lease REIT W. P. Carey (NYSE:WPC) was upgraded at BMO Capital Markets on factors such as growth and incremental cost of capital.

WPC shares were trading +0.56% Tuesday pre-market at $59.02.

"After two years of declining earnings driven by office dispositions, WPC is back to delivering AFFOps growth, with 2025 guidance midpoint of $4.87 (+3.6% y/y), above consensus," said analysts John Kim and Eric Borden in a research note.

"WPC has unique organic growth among net lease REITs," said the note.

The company's incremental cost of capital is advantageous, allowing it to spread invest attractively, according to the research note.

BMO Capital Markets raised its recommendation on the stock to Outperform from Market Perform, and increased the price target to $67 from $60.

The average sell-side analyst rating on the stock is Hold. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha authors and the Quant Rating system grade WPC as Buy.

The stock has risen 5.12% in the last one year.

data.title: Anglo American to sell Brazil nickel business for up to $500M, keep 20% of demerged platinum unit
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said Tuesday it agreed to sell its nickel business in Brazil to Hong Kong-listed MMG Ltd. for as much as $500M, the latest step in the company's broader restructuring aimed at refocusing its operations on copper and iron ore mining.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said the deal comprises a $350M up-front cash payment at completion, up to $100M in a price-linked earnout and a contingent $50M in cash for the potential development of the projects.

Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) nickel business includes two ferronickel operations and two growth projects in Brazil, producing a combined ~38K metric tons/year of nickel.

Ahead of its financial results on February 20, Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) also said the spinoff of its platinum unit in South Africa will be completed by June; the company plans to keep a 19.9% stake but will not hold any representation on Anglo American Platinum's (OTCPK:ANGPY) (OTCPK:AGPPF) board after the split.

The miner, which already disposed of its steelmaking coal business, also is trying to sell its De Beers diamond unit, but the business is proving hard to sell and is expected to book an impairment on its full-year results.

Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) decision to retain a stake after the demerger is not the clean break analysts had expected, RBC Capital analysts Ben Davis and Marina Calero wrote.

data.title: Odd Burger CEO purchases 50,000 company shares
data.description:
• Odd Burger (TSXV:ODD:CA) said on Tuesday that Co-founder and Chief Operating Officer, Vasiliki McInnes purchased 50,000 shares of the company at an average price of $0.3231 per share.
  • As a result of the acquisition, McInnes will now own and exercise control over an ‎aggregate of ‎‎22.46M ‎‎common shares, 1.84M options ‎and 892,857 warrants representing ‎approximately 22.8% ‎of the issued ‎and outstanding voting ‎securities of the ‎‎issuer on a non-diluted ‎basis and 25.6% of the ‎issued and outstanding securities of ‎the issuer, ‎‎assuming conversion of the ‎options and ‎exercise of the warrants‎. ‎
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock futures higher as Wall Street kicks off holiday-shortened week
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were higher on Tuesday with attention on geopolitics.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were +0.46% in pre-market trading, while benchmark S&P futures (SPX) were +0.39%. Dow futures (INDU) were +0.19%.

There was more action in the bond market as global rates rose. European bonds were the driver, with prices falling on speculation that the market would see new issuance as countries spent more on defense.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.53% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 1 basis point to 4.29%.

The U.S. and Russia peace talks over Ukraine are set to begin today in Saudi Arabia. But Ukraine and European nations will not be present.

Deutsche Bank strategist Jim Reid said "potential implications are huge after the events of the last five days where first the US froze out Europe in announcing the start of talks with Russia, and second with the broadside against Europe from US Vice President JD Vance."

"That all this is occurring within a week of the German general election (this Sunday) means that Europe might start to move down a very different path within weeks to the pre-existing one."

On the macro front, the February Empire State Manufacturing Index surprised to the upside.

"The initial reaction (Monday) was to price in more European defense spending with bonds selling off and equities rising, especially in the defense sector," Reid said.

data.title: ProPhase Labs hires former Barstool Sports COO Stuart Hollenshead, plans cost-cutting measures
data.description:
ProPhase Labs (NASDAQ:PRPH) Tuesday named Stu Hollenshead as Chief Operating Officer, marking a pivotal step in the company’s expansion into consumer-centered health and wellness products.

ProPhase Labs (NASDAQ:PRPH) said it plans to provide additional updates very shortly regarding progress in its accounts receivables that the company believes will be significant, the exploration of new strategic alternatives for Nebula Genomics and DNA Complete, and additional cost-cutting measures.

“The company’s particular focus going forward will be direct-to-consumer revenue streams," Ted Karkus, CEO of ProPhase Labs said.

Furthermore, Karkus said the company is "optimistic about the potential for a significant inflow of capital into the company between Q2 and Q3 2025" related to enhanced accounts receivable collection initiatives.

"These are just some of the reasons that someone of Stu’s caliber was willing to transition from being a consultant at ProPhase to its COO," Karkus added.

Hollenshead served as Chief Operating Officer & Chief Business Officer at Barstool Sports, leading the company through record-breaking expansion in revenue, audience, and product monetization—culminating in its $551-million acquisition by Penn Entertainment in 2023.
